title: Cánh Gà Chiên

groups.heading: Gà · Wings
items: 8 chicken wings, split at the joints (tips removed)

sections.heading: Cách làm · Instructions
steps: Marinate the wings: toss them with salt, fish sauce, sugar, sesame oil, and minced garlic. Let them sit for about 30 minutes.
When ready to fry, pat the wings dry and coat each one in cornstarch. Shake off the excess.
Heat oil in a deep pot or wok to 350°F. Fry the wings in batches — don't crowd the pot — for about 8–10 minutes, until golden brown and cooked through. Drain on a wire rack or paper towels.

While the wings fry, make the glaze: in a small saucepan, heat 1 tablespoon oil over medium heat. Sauté the garlic until fragrant. Add water, chicken broth, paprika, sugar, dark corn syrup, salt, pepper, and soy sauce. Stir and simmer for 3–4 minutes until the sauce thickens and becomes glossy.
Toss the hot fried wings in the glaze, or pour the glaze over the wings on a serving plate. Make sure each wing is coated in that sticky, sweet-savory sauce.

scan_label: Công thức gốc · Original handwritten recipe
